name: Hopewell, NJ
description: Home base — a small historic borough in Mercer County, New Jersey.

name: Yale University
description: One of the world's leading research universities, founded in 1701. Home to the Yale School of Drama, Beinecke Rare Book Library, and a stunning Gothic campus in New Haven, CT.

name: Harvard University
description: The oldest university in the United States, founded in 1636. Located in Cambridge, MA, Harvard has produced 8 U.S. presidents, 161 Nobel laureates, and countless groundbreaking scholars.

name: MIT — Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: A world-renowned science and technology university in Cambridge, MA. MIT's campus sits along the Charles River and is home to 97 Nobel laureates and countless innovations.

name: Dudley Café
description: A beloved community gathering spot in Roxbury, one of Boston's most historically Black neighborhoods. Great coffee, food, and local art.

name: Sofra Bakery & Café
description: An acclaimed Middle Eastern bakery and café in Cambridge. Pastries, mezze spreads, and strong coffee — beloved by Harvard and MIT communities alike.

name: Museum of African American History
description: The largest institution in New England dedicated to preserving and sharing the contributions of African Americans. Located on Beacon Hill, it includes the African Meeting House — the oldest surviving Black church in the US.

name: Stowe Mountain Resort
description: Vermont's premier ski destination. Mt. Mansfield — Vermont's highest peak — offers terrain for all levels. Stowe village is charming and walkable.

name: Killington Resort
description: The largest ski resort in the Eastern US with 155 trails. A classic spring skiing destination — often open into May. Great for beginners and thrill-seekers alike.

name: Mont Tremblant
description: Quebec's most famous ski resort, with a picturesque pedestrian village at its base. 102 trails, beginner-friendly schools, and a distinctly French Canadian atmosphere.
address: 1000 Chemin des Voyageurs, Mont-Tremblant, QC J8E 1T1

name: Little Burgundy, Montreal
description: Montreal's historically Black neighborhood, home to the vibrant jazz heritage of Oscar Peterson and the famous Joe Beef restaurant strip. Rich with Caribbean and African diaspora culture.

name: Chez Aline
description: A beloved Haitian restaurant in Montreal serving traditional dishes like griot, diri ak pwa, and tasso. A taste of the Caribbean diaspora in Quebec.

name: Portland, ME — Old Port Waterfront
description: Portland's historic waterfront district with cobblestone streets, working piers, seafood shacks, and boutique shops. A quintessential Maine coastal experience.

name: Tesla Supercharger — Hartford, CT
description: Tesla Supercharger station near I-91 in Hartford, CT. Convenient stop between Hopewell NJ and New Haven.

name: Tesla Supercharger — Boston, MA
description: Tesla Supercharger in the Boston area.

name: Tesla Supercharger — Montpelier, VT
description: Tesla Supercharger near I-89 in Montpelier, VT. Key stop on the Vermont ski leg.

name: Faneuil Hall Marketplace
description: Boston's iconic marketplace and meeting hall, built in 1742. Today it's a vibrant tourist hub with food vendors, street performers, and shops in the heart of downtown Boston. The Great Hall upstairs is a living piece of American history — Frederick Douglass, Susan B. Anthony, and countless others spoke here.

name: Freedom Trail
description: A 2.5-mile red-brick trail connecting 16 historically significant sites through downtown Boston and Charlestown. Includes the Massachusetts State House, Paul Revere House, Old North Church, and the Bunker Hill Monument. One of America's most important historic walking trails.
address: Boston Common Visitor Center, 139 Tremont St, Boston, MA 02111

name: Boston Common & Public Garden
description: America's oldest public park (1634), in the heart of downtown Boston. Adjacent to the beautiful Public Garden with swan boats and weeping willows. A perfect place to decompress between museum visits and campus tours.

name: Boston Waterfront & Harborwalk
description: Boston's scenic 47-mile harborwalk along the waterfront. The Fort Point Channel and Seaport District stretch is especially vibrant — great seafood, the Institute of Contemporary Art, and sweeping harbor views.

name: Old North Church
description: Boston's oldest surviving church (1723), famous for the "one if by land, two if by sea" signal lanterns that warned Paul Revere of the British advance. A cornerstone of the Freedom Trail.

name: Old Montréal (Vieux-Montréal)
description: Montréal's historic heart, with cobblestone streets, 17th–18th century architecture, the Old Port, and the iconic Bonsecours Market. Place Jacques-Cartier is the social center. A world away from modern Montréal — and one of the most beautiful historic districts in North America.

name: Notre-Dame Basilica de Montréal
description: One of North America's most breathtaking interiors — a neo-Gothic masterpiece with a vaulted blue ceiling studded with gold stars. Celine Dion got married here. The AURA light show runs evenings. Located in Old Montréal.
address: 110 Rue Notre-Dame O, Montréal, QC H2Y 1T2

name: Mount Royal Park
description: Designed by Frederick Law Olmsted (who designed Central Park), Mount Royal is Montréal's beloved forested hilltop park. The Kondiaronk Belvedere offers a sweeping panoramic view of the entire city skyline. A perfect half-day escape.

name: McGill University
description: One of Canada's top universities, founded in 1821. Its stunning Victorian campus sits at the base of Mount Royal. Walking up the main gates and through the Arts Building quad is free and gives a real feel for one of North America's great research universities.
address: 845 Rue Sherbrooke O, Montréal, QC H3A 0G4

name: Jean-Talon Market
description: The largest open-air market in North America, in the heart of Montréal's Little Italy. Produces, spices, local cheeses, Québécois maple products, and incredible food stalls. A vibrant, multicultural sensory experience.

name: Brown University
description: An Ivy League university in Providence, RI, founded in 1764. Known for its open curriculum, creative culture, and stunning College Hill campus. Brown has produced Pulitzer Prize winners, tech founders, and senators — and its campus is one of the most photogenic in New England.

name: RISD Museum of Art
description: One of the finest university art museums in the country, attached to the Rhode Island School of Design. 91,000 objects spanning ancient Egyptian artifacts to contemporary works. Affordable, uncrowded, and genuinely world-class.

name: Federal Hill, Providence
description: Providence's Italian neighborhood — one of the best in New England. Atwells Avenue is lined with restaurants, bakeries, and the iconic pine cone arch at the entrance. A perfect dinner spot the night before heading to Boston.

name: Providence, RI
description: Rhode Island's capital — a compact, creative city with two major universities, a thriving food scene, and a beautiful colonial waterfront. An underrated overnight stop between New Jersey and Boston.

name: Tesla Supercharger — Providence, RI
description: Tesla Supercharger in the Providence, RI area. Useful on the Hopewell → Providence leg.

name: Tesla Supercharger — New Haven, CT
description: Tesla Supercharger in New Haven, CT. Useful for top-off during a Yale stop.

name: Cottage — Hadley, NY
description: Family cottage in Hadley, NY — at the southern gateway to the Adirondacks, near the Great Sacandaga Lake. A perfect overnight stop to break up the long drive home from Montréal.

name: Tesla Supercharger — Plattsburgh, NY
description: Tesla Supercharger near I-87 in Plattsburgh, NY. Key stop on the Montréal-to-Adirondacks corridor, about an hour south of the Canadian border.

name: Tesla Supercharger — Albany, NY
description: Tesla Supercharger near I-87 in the Albany, NY area. Convenient stop between the Adirondacks and the New York metro area.

name: Boston Harbor Cruises — Historic Sightseeing
description: Classic 90-minute narrated harbor cruise departing from Long Wharf, right next to the New England Aquarium. See the Boston skyline, harbor islands, and lighthouses from the water. Runs daily spring through fall.

name: Le Bateau-Mouche — Montréal River Cruise
description: A glass-enclosed river cruise along the St. Lawrence from the Old Port of Montréal. Modeled after the famous Paris Bateau-Mouche fleet. Day cruises, brunch cruises, and sunset options available. Beautiful views of Old Montréal, the Jacques Cartier Bridge, and Île Sainte-Hélène.
address: Jacques-Cartier Pier, Old Port, Montréal, QC H2Y 2E2
